nodes.name: Telegram Bot Trigger
nodes.notes: Listens for messages sent to your Telegram bot. Requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN credential.

nodes.name: Authorised User?
nodes.notes: CRITICAL: Only responds to your Telegram user ID. Set TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_ID in n8n Variables. Find your ID at @userinfobot on Telegram.

nodes.name: Get Container Status
nodes.notes: Gets status from Uptime Kuma API. Alternative: use a Code node to run 'docker ps' via SSH.

parameters.text:
=📋 *Recent n8n Executions*

Check execution logs in your n8n dashboard:
https://workflow.{{ $vars.BASE_DOMAIN }}/executions

_Direct log streaming requires SSH access._

⚠️ *Restart not available via Telegram*

For safety, service restarts require SSH access.

SSH: `ssh root@{{ $vars.SERVER_IP }}`
Then: `cd /opt/technomancer/docker && docker compose restart [service]`

nodes.name: Send Restart Notice
nodes.notes: Restart via Telegram is disabled for safety — it would require giving n8n SSH access, which is a significant security risk. Direct users to SSH.

meta.description: Telegram bot interface for basic server status queries. Authorisation is enforced by matching the sender's Telegram user ID to a known-good value. Restart commands are intentionally blocked — they require SSH for safety.
meta.chapter_reference: Part II, Chapter 8 — The Self-Hosted Sovereign
meta.security_note: CRITICAL: Set TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_ID before activating this workflow. Without it, anyone who knows your bot token can query your server status.
meta.setup_instructions: 1. Create a Telegram Bot via @BotFather. Get the token. 2. Find your Telegram user ID via @userinfobot. 3. In n8n: add Telegram Bot credential with your token. 4. Set TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_ID variable to your user ID. 5. Set BASE_DOMAIN and SERVER_IP variables. 6. Activate workflow. 7. Send /help to your bot.
